original_question:
Tom, Dick and Harry started out on a $100$-mile journey. Tom and Harry went by automobile at the rate of $25$ mph, while Dick walked at the rate of $5$ mph. After a certain distance, Harry got off and walked on at $5$ mph, while Tom went back for Dick and got him to the destination at the same time that Harry arrived. Calculate the number of hours required for the trip.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

entries.original_incomplete_question: John, Paul and George started out on a [BLANK] journey. John and George went by automobile at the rate of 40 mph, while Paul walked at the rate of 4 mph. After a certain distance, George got off and walked on at 4 mph, while John went back for Paul and got him to the destination at the same time that George arrived. Calculate the number of hours required for the trip.

variants.variant: John, Paul, and George embarked on a [BLANK] journey. John and George traveled by car at a speed of 40 mph, whereas Paul walked at a pace of 4 mph. After covering a certain distance, George disembarked and continued on foot at 4 mph, while John returned to fetch Paul and brought him to the destination at the same moment that George arrived. Determine the total hours needed for the trip.

John, Paul, and George began a [BLANK] journey together. John and George drove in a vehicle at 40 mph, while Paul made his way on foot at 4 mph. After some distance, George exited and proceeded to walk at 4 mph, while John went back to pick up Paul, arriving at the destination simultaneously with George. Find out the number of hours taken for the journey.

John, Paul, and George set off on a [BLANK] journey. John and George used a car traveling at 40 mph, while Paul walked at 4 mph. After a particular distance, George left the car and continued walking at 4 mph, while John returned to get Paul and managed to reach the destination at the same time as George. Calculate how many hours the trip took.

After traveling a certain distance, George got out and walked at 4 mph, while John went back for Paul and brought him to the destination right when George arrived. Calculate the hours needed for this trip.

After some distance, George exited the vehicle and continued on foot at 4 mph, while John returned to pick up Paul, reaching the destination at the same time as George.
